title: Crunchy Slovakian Olives Salad
description: A fresh, crunchy salad that brings Slovakian flavors to a weeknight table, featuring olives, deglazed onions, and a burst of lemon. It’s low-sodium-friendly and ready in just a few minutes.
instructions: Heat a non-stick skillet over medium heat and add the olive oil.
Add the sliced red onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Pour in the white wine to deglaze, scraping any browned bits from the pan; cook until the liquid reduces by half, about 2 minutes.
Transfer the deglazed onion mixture to a bowl and let it cool to room temperature.
In a large salad bowl, combine the sliced olives, cherry tomatoes, diced cucumber, capers, and toasted pine nuts.
Add the cooled onion mixture, lemon juice, chopped dill, and a pinch of black pepper; toss gently to combine.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed, keeping sodium low by avoiding added salt.
Serve immediately for a crunchy, fresh bite of Slovakian-inspired flavor.
